questions.question: The number of moles of solute in 10 Liters of a 0.344 M solution is:
At 379 K, the equilibrium constant Kp for a reaction is 6. If the temperature is increased, favoring the endothermic direction, what happens to Kp?
questions.solution: For endothermic reactions, increasing temperature increases the equilibrium constant.

questions.question: Which of the following follows Law of definite proportions?

options.A: Pure water from any source always contains H and O in 2:16 mass ratio

questions.question: Which of the following alkyl halides undergoes SN1 reaction most rapidly under identical conditions? (Solvent: aqueous ethanol, 20°C)
questions.hint: SN1 mechanisms proceed via carbocation intermediates. The more stable the carbocation, the faster the reaction.
questions.solution: SN1 rate depends on carbocation stability (3° > 2° > 1°). Option A forms a highly stable tertiary carbocation.
